description: Registry and scene-file contract checks for frame-pure scenes.

scenarios.story: As a contributor, I want a valid scene to load automatically through the registry.
scenarios.given: A new scene file exports a function whose name matches the basename and the registry has META + render entries.
then: REGISTRY contains the new scene id
META exposes id/category/description/duration_hint/params
No runtime contract error is thrown

scenarios.story: As a contributor, I want missing describe support to fail at startup.
scenarios.given: A registry entry is built without a describe function.
then: SceneContractError is thrown
The error names the broken scene id
The error message points at describe missing
scenarios.critical: Broken scenes must fail on import, not during a later render

scenarios.story: As a maintainer, I want forbidden non-deterministic tokens blocked by architecture tests.
scenarios.given: A scene file contains Math.random or Date.now or performance.now.
then: arch-2 fails
The failing scene file is named
The forbidden token is shown in the assertion message

scenarios.story: As an AI tool user, I want every scene to publish complete param metadata.
scenarios.given: META_TABLE contains one entry per scene id.
then: Each META has category and description
duration_hint is numeric
params is an array of specs with name/type/default

scenarios.story: As a contributor, I want a new scene to be auto-discovered by tests once I wire the registry entry.
scenarios.given: The new scene file exists and index.js imports it.
then: The new scene id appears in SCENE_IDS
REGISTRY has a valid entry
No unrelated files need edits

scenarios.story: As a maintainer, I want duplicate scene ids rejected quickly.
scenarios.given: Two registry entries try to publish the same id.
then: The duplicate is surfaced immediately
Tests fail before render
The error points at the colliding id
scenarios.note: Current implementation relies on code review plus registry import tests; a stricter duplicate guard can land later

scenarios.story: As a contributor, I want incomplete META to fail the contract.
scenarios.given: A scene META omits category or params.
then: SceneContractError is thrown
The missing META field is named
Import aborts before any CLI command runs
